title: Async Fetch-to-Commit Orchestration, Results, and Side-Effect Ordering
description: Nested promises mutate shared state, validators save before entries, half-imports become visible, telemetry reports success before commit, and post-commit work can duplicate. Learners cumulatively produce atomic typed ingestion orchestration through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent claim, fetch, parse, validate, normalize, commit, publish, fail, cancel, and supersede states as exhaustive discriminated results.
Pass immutable stage outputs and commit only one admitted unit rather than mutating a shared feed object across awaits.
Commit feed, entries, revisions, validators, schedule, lease, and audit evidence together or retain the previous admitted version.
